hooks.command:
INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if ! echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'mvnw|full-check|verify|spotless|smoke-startup|doc-lint|fast-check'; then exit 0; fi; REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel 2>/dev/null || pwd)"; MSG=''; for plan in "$REPO_ROOT"/docs/exec-plans/active/*.md; do [ -e "$plan" ] || continue; NAME=$(basename "$plan"); OPEN=$(grep -c '^- \[ \]' "$plan" 2>/dev/null); OPEN=${OPEN:-0}; DONE=$(grep -c '^- \[x\]' "$plan" 2>/dev/null); DONE=${DONE:-0}; if [ "$OPEN" -gt 0 ]; then MSG="$MSG  - $NAME ($DONE done, $OPEN remaining — review and mark completed items [x])
"; elif [ "$DONE" -gt 0 ]; then MSG="$MSG  - $NAME (all items checked — move to docs/exec-plans/completed/)
"; fi; done; if [ -n "$MSG" ]; then printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PostToolUse","additionalContext":"EXEC PLAN CHECKPOINT: Active plans in docs/exec-plans/active/:
%s"}}
' "$MSG"; fi

hooks.prompt: Before exiting plan mode: does your plan touch 3+ files or involve architectural changes? If yes, check docs/exec-plans/active/ — if no relevant exec plan exists, create one with scripts/harness/new-exec-plan before proceeding. Plan mode is for conversation alignment; exec plans are the persistent project record.

If you wrote or modified tests in this task: verify they test behavior, not implementation. Would the test still fail if the feature were reimplemented with a completely different algorithm? If the test mirrors your implementation logic, rewrite it.
